audit_dimensions: JP kana leak into ZH fields
Duplicate surface terms (same vs cross-page restatement)
Safety/security concept correctness (9 security-specific terms checked)
Empty / suspiciously short definitions (false positives confirmed)
ZH translation consistency across duplicate JP sources

findings.description: リスクマネジメント (Risk Management) appears as a full term entity on both p515 and p516 with word-for-word identical JP definition. This is a release-impacting duplicate: learners will see the same term defined twice in sequential pages.
findings.evidence: defn.jp identical: '会社の活動に伴って発生するあらゆるリスクを管理し、そのリスクによる損失を最小の費用で食い止めるためのプロセス。'
findings.recommendation: Deduplicate: retain one canonical entry (p515 or p516), remove the other. If both pages need the term, use a cross-reference or stub pointer.

findings.description: The two duplicate リスクマネジメント entries use different ZH wording for identical JP source text: p515 says '以最低成本控制由该风险带来的损失的过程' while p516 says '以最低成本遏制风险造成损失的流程'. Both are semantically correct but inconsistent.
findings.evidence: p515 zh ends '…损失的过程', p516 zh ends '…损失的流程'
findings.recommendation: Resolve with F01 deduplication. Adopt one ZH rendering canonically.

findings.description: Section heading 'リスクマネジメント' appears on both p515 and p517. Two separate section-type entities carry the same JP title. This may confuse table-of-contents generation or navigation.
findings.evidence: itpassport_r6::section::p515::0 title.jp='リスクマネジメント', itpassport_r6::section::p517::0 title.jp='リスクマネジメント'
findings.recommendation: Verify whether these are genuinely two subsections of the same topic (sub-numbering missing) or a pipeline section-detection duplicate.

findings.description: DDoS攻撃 EN definition ('An attack that overloads a system by repeatedly sending massive amounts of access requests from many computers via the Internet to a targeted server.') does not expand the DDoS acronym to 'Distributed Denial of Service'. The surface.en is only 'DDoS Attack'. Learners reading only the EN definition cannot decode the abbreviation.
findings.evidence: surface.en='DDoS Attack'; defn.en does not contain 'Distributed' or 'Denial of Service'
findings.recommendation: Prepend expansion: 'DDoS (Distributed Denial of Service) Attack is an attack that…'

findings.description: 5 security terms appear as both full-form definitions (p512) and abbreviated summary entries on later pages (p513/p514). Content differs (full vs short). These are intentional pedagogical restatements (summary boxes), not copy errors. No content defect detected.
findings.evidence: DoS攻撃, 総当たり攻撃, 辞書攻撃, パスワードリスト攻撃, クロスサイトスクリプティング each appear twice with differing definition lengths
findings.recommendation: INFO only. Confirm pipeline is aware these are intentional summary variants, not duplicates to deduplicate.

clean_checks: JP kana in ZH fields: 0 instances
ZH surface identical to JP: 0 instances
Empty definitions: 0 (2 short-form entries confirmed accurate translations of brief JP originals)
Table cell completeness: PASS (3 tables, 15 rows total, no empty cells, no encoding issues)
Security concept accuracy: 9 terms checked; DoS/XSS/Ransomware/Phishing/SQLi all correctly translated
summary: CHUNK_50 MOSTLY_PASS with 1 HIGH + 3 MEDIUM + 1 LOW finding. No CRITICAL defects (no U+FFFD, no kana-in-ZH, no empty definitions). Primary issue: リスクマネジメント (Risk Management) duplicated as identical entities on p515 and p516 [F01, HIGH], with a secondary ZH inconsistency between the two copies [F02, MEDIUM]. DDoS acronym unexpanded in EN definition [F04, MEDIUM]. Duplicate section heading on p515 and p517 [F03, MEDIUM]. 5 cross-page summary restatements are pedagogical design, not defects [F05, LOW]. Security/safety concept translations for all 20 checked terms are semantically correct.
